mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns exceed 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate output and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances during tightening cycles, or the signal predictive power vanishes when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 310, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310, or the threshold effect disappears in a quantile regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or the Rank IC falls below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a liquidity tightening phase signals supply chain bottlenecks and reduced factory output, which depresses corporate margins and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of contraction months during tightening phases, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 in out-of-sample tests.
